Question:
Statement I: In frogs, the kidneys are compact, dark red and bean like structures situated a little anteriorly in the body cavity on both sides of vertebral column
Statement II: Frog has different types of sense organs, namely organs of touch (sensory papillae), taste (taste buds), smell (nasal epithelium), vision (eyes) and hearing (tympanum with internal ears); out of which eyes and internal ears are cellular aggregations around nerve endings
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution:
Statement I: In frogs, the kidneys are compact, dark red and bean like structures situated a little posteriorly in the body cavity on both sides of vertebral column
Statement II: Frog has different types of sense organs, namely organs of touch (sensory papillae), taste (taste buds), smell (nasal epithelium), vision (eyes) and hearing (tympanum with internal ears); out of which eyes and internal ears are well-organised structures and the rest are cellular aggregations around nerve endings

Question: Consider the flowchart given below and identify A, B and C:
Options: A-Adenohypophysis, B-Pars nervosa, C-Pars distalis
A-Hypophysis, B-Pars distalis, C-Pars nervosa
A-Posteriohypophysis, B-Pars nervosa, C-Pars distalis
A-Adenohypophysis, B-Pars distalis, C-Pars nervosa
Solution:
The correct labelling is: A: Adenohypophysis; B: Pars distalis; C: Pars nervosa
Pituitary gland is divided anatomically into an adenohypophysis and a neurohypophysis. Adenohypophysis consists of two portions, pars distalis and pars intermedia. The pars distalis region of pituitary, commonly called anterior pituitary.
Neurohypophysis (pars nervosa) is also known as posterior pituitary.

Question:

Question:

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct set of statements:
Statement I: The glycocalyx is the outermost component of the bacterial cell envelope.
Statement II: If the glycocalyx is a loose sheath, it is called the capsule
Statement III: If the glycocalyx is thick and tough, it is called the slime layer
Statement IV: The cell wall determines the shape of the cell and provides a strong structural support to prevent the bacterium from bursting or collapsing
Options: Statements I, III, and IV are correct
Statements I, II, and IV are correct
Statements I and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution:
Statement I is Correct: Glycocalyx is the outermost layer of the cell envelope.
Statement II is Incorrect: A loose sheath is called a slime layer.
Statement III is Incorrect: A thick and tough glycocalyx is called a capsule.
Statement IV is Correct: This is the primary function of the bacterial cell wall, which lies between the glycocalyx and the plasma membrane.

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer:
Statement I: In human beings, after one month of pregnancy, the embryo’s heart is formed.
Statement II: Immediately after implantation, the inner cell mass (embryo) differentiates into an outer layer called endoderm and an inner layer called ectoderm
Statement III: Two germ layers – ectoderm and endoderm give rise to all tissues (organs) in human adults
Statement IV: Breast-feeding during the initial period of infant growth is recommended by doctors for bringing up a healthy baby
Options: Statements I, II and III are correct
Statements I and III are correct
Statements II and III are not incorrect
Statements I and IV are not incorrect
Solution:
Statement II is incorrect: Immediately after implantation, the inner cell mass (embryo) differentiates into an outer layer called ectoderm and an inner layer called endoderm
Statement III: Three germ layers- ectoderm, endoderm and mesoderm give rise to all tissues (organs) in adults

Question:

Statement IV: The milk produced during the initial few days of lactation is called colostrum which contains several antibodies (IgA) essential to develop resistance for the new-born babies

I-T, II-T, III-F, IV-F

I-F, II-T, III-F, IV-T
Solution: Statement II is False: Oxytocin (not relaxin) is the hormone responsible for strong uterine contractions. Relaxin, as the name suggests, helps in relaxing the pelvic ligaments.

Question:

Question:

Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect

Question:
Assertion (A): In a dorsiventral leaf, the size of the vascular bundles varies
Reason (R): Dorsiventral leaves possess reticulate venation where the veins vary in thickness
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: The Assertion is correct. In dicot leaves, vascular bundles are not all the same size (unlike monocot leaves). The Reason correctly explains this: the size of the vascular bundle is dependent on the size of the vein, and since reticulate venation has a network of veins with varying thicknesses (midrib, lateral veins, veinlets), the bundles vary accordingly.

Question:
Statement I: The Law of Independent Assortment states that when two pairs of traits are combined in a hybrid, segregation of one pair of characters is independent of the other pair of characters
Statement II: This law holds true for genes that are located on the same chromosome in very close physical proximity
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is Incorrect: Genes located very close on the same chromosome show linkage and do not assort independently. The law applies to genes on different chromosomes or those far apart on the same chromosome.

Question:

Question:
Consider the following statements regarding the phases and duration of the cell cycle:
Statement I: The Interphase lasts for more than 95% of the duration of the cell cycle

Statement II: In the 24-hour average duration of cell cycle of a human cell, cell division proper lasts for 2-3hrs

Statement III: Yeast cells can progress through the cell cycle in only about 90 minutes.

Statement IV: Some cells in the adult animals do not appear to exhibit division e.g., skin cells

How many of the above statements are CORRECT?
Options: One
Four
Two
Three
Solution:
Statement II is Incorrect: In the 24-hour average duration of cell cycle of a human cell, cell division proper lasts for only about an hour.
Statement IV is Incorrect: Some cells in the adult animals do not appear to exhibit division e.g., Heart cells.

Question:

Question:
Assertion (A): In the transverse section of a dicot stem, the vascular bundles are arranged in a ring
Reason (R): The roots of dicotyledonous plants show an exarch condition of xylem
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both are true anatomical facts, but the arrangement of bundles in the stem is not caused or explained by the xylem arrangement in the root.

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct option:
Statement I: Besides acting as ‘conduits’ for energy transfer across trophic levels, predators play other important roles like keeping prey populations under control
Statement II: Brood parasitism in birds like crows is not a true example of parasitism in which the parasitic bird lays its eggs in the nest of its host and lets the host incubate them
Statement III: Gause’s ‘Competitive Exclusion Principle’ states that two closely related species competing for the same resources can coexist indefinitely
Statement IV: Some species facing competition might evolve mechanisms like ‘resource partitioning’ to promote coexistence rather than exclusion
Options: Statements I, II, and IV are correct
Statements II and III are incorrect
Statements I and III are correct
All statements are correct
Solution:
Statement II is incorrect: Brood parasitism in birds like cuckoo (koel) is a fascinating example of parasitism in which the parasitic bird lays its eggs in the nest of its host (crow) and lets the host incubate them.
Statement III is incorrect because Gause’s Principle states that two closely related species competing for the same resources cannot coexist indefinitely and the competitively inferior one will be eliminated eventually (provided resources are limiting).

Question: Which experiment is shown in the figure?
Options: Griffith’s experiment
Avery, MacLeod and McCarty
Hershey-Chase experiment
Meselson and Stahl experiment
Solution: The correct matching is: Hershey-Chase experiment

Question:
Statement I: The hypothalamus contains a number of centres which control body temperature and the urge for eating and drinking
Statement II: The hypothalamus is located above the thalamus
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is Incorrect: The hypothalamus lies at the base (ventral side) of the thalamus, not above it.

Question:
After studying the following statements select the correct answer among the following
Statement I: DNA fingerprinting involves identifying differences in some specific regions in DNA sequence called repetitive DNA
Statement II: These repetitive DNA are separated from bulk genomic DNA as different peaks during density gradient centrifugation
Statement III: The bulk DNA forms a small peak and the other major peaks are referred to as satellite DNA
Statement IV: Satellite DNA is classified into categories like micro-satellites and mini-satellites based on base composition, length of segment, and number of repetitive units
Options: Statements I, II, and IV are correct
Statements I and III are correct
Statements II and III are correct
All statements are correct
Solution: Statement III is incorrect: The bulk DNA forms a major peak and the other small peaks are referred to as satellite DNA.

Question: Match the property in Column I with its specific requirement in Column II:
Options: A-iv, B-iii, C-ii, D-i
A-ii, B-i, C-iv, D-iii
A-ii, B-iv, C-i, D-iii
A-i, B-ii, C-iii, D-iv
Solution: The correct matching is: A-ii, B-iv, C-i, D-iii

Question:
Match the Darwinian term in Column I with its meaning in Column II:
Column IColumn II
A.Biological Fitness(i)Determined by the life cycle/span
B.Natural Selection(ii)Ultimate result is reproductive success
C.Rate of Evolution(iii)Nature selects for those better adapted
D.Adaptive Ability(iv)Inherited character that helps survival
Options: A-iii, B-iv, C-ii, D-i
A-iv, B-i, C-iii, D-ii
A-ii, B-iii, C-i, D-iv
A-i, B-ii, C-iv, D-iii
Solution: The correct matching is: A-ii, B-iii, C-i, D-iv

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer:
Statement I: For effective treatment of a disease, early diagnosis and understanding its pathophysiology is very important
Statement II: Using conventional methods of diagnosis (serum and urine analysis), early detection of pathogens is not possible
Statement III: PCR is now routinely used to detect HIV in suspected AIDS patients and to detect mutations in genes in suspected cancer patients
Statement IV: Recombinant DNA technology and ELISA are conventional diagnostic techniques used for late-stage detection of diseases
Options: Statements I, II, and III are correct
Statements I and III are correct
Statements II, III, and IV are correct
Statements I, III, and IV are correct
Solution: Statement IV is incorrect: Recombinant DNA technology, PCR, and ELISA are Molecular Diagnostic techniques, not "conventional" ones. They allow for early detection even when the concentration of the pathogen is very low.

Question:
Assertion (A): The number of mammalian species in the world is more than the combined total of the species of fishes, amphibians, reptiles and fungi
Reason (R): For many taxonomic groups, species inventories are more complete in temperate than in tropical countries
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Assertion is false: The number of fungi species in the world is more than the combined total of the species of fishes, amphibians, reptiles and mammals

Question:
Assertion (A): The clavicle is a long slender bone with two curvatures
Reason (R): The scapula is a large triangular flat bone situated in the dorsal part of the thorax between the second and the seventh ribs
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both statements are factually correct descriptions of bones in the pectoral girdle. However, the shape/location of the scapula (Reason) does not explain why the clavicle has two curvatures (Assertion). They are independent facts.

Question:
Assertion (A): Hirudinaria possesses lateral appendages called parapodia which help in swimming
Reason (R): Annelids possess longitudinal and circular muscles which help in locomotion
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Assertion is Incorrect: Hirudinaria (Leeches) do not possess parapodia. Parapodia are found in aquatic annelids like Nereis.

Question:
After studying the following statements select the correct answer among the following:
Statement I: Selectable markers help in identifying and eliminating non-transformants and selectively permitting the growth of transformants
Statement II: Plasmids because of their high number per cell, have very high copy numbers of their genome within the bacterial cells
Statement III: The genes encoding resistance to antibiotics such as ampicillin and chloramphenicol are considered useful selectable markers for E. coli
Statement IV: Normal E. coli cells naturally carry resistance against most common antibiotics like tetracycline
Options: Statements I and II are correct
Statements I, II, and III are correct
Statements III and IV are correct
Statements I and III are correct
Solution:
Statement II is incorrect: Bacteriophages because of their high number per cell, have very high copy numbers of their genome within the bacterial cells
Statement IV is incorrect: Normal E. coli cells do not carry resistance against any of these antibiotics; the resistance genes must be introduced via a vector.

Question:
Statement I: The canal called the cerebral aqueduct passes through the forebrain
Statement II: The dorsal portion of the midbrain consists mainly of four round swellings (lobes) called corpora quadrigemina
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement I is Incorrect: The cerebral aqueduct passes through the midbrain, not the forebrain.

Question:
Statement I: The systemic circulation begins with the pumping of oxygenated blood by the left ventricle into the aorta
Statement II: The pulmonary circulation begins with the pumping of deoxygenated blood by the left atrium into the pulmonary artery
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is Incorrect: Pulmonary circulation starts at the Right Ventricle, which pumps deoxygenated blood into the pulmonary artery. The left atrium receives oxygenated blood.

Question:
Consider the following four statements regarding the electrical potential of the axonal membrane:
Statement 1: In a resting neuron, the axonal membrane is comparatively more permeable to potassium ions and nearly impermeable to sodium ions
Statement 2: The ionic gradients across the resting membrane are maintained by the active transport of ions by the Na+-K+ pump which transports 3Na+ outwards for 2K+ inwards
Statement 3: When a stimulus is applied, the membrane at that site becomes freely permeable to K+ leading to a rapid influx of K+ and reversal of polarity
Statement 4: The rise in stimulus-induced permeability to Na+ is extremely short-lived and is quickly followed by a rise in permeability to K+
Which of the following combinations of statements is correct?
Options: 1, 2 and 4
1 and 2 only
2, 3 and 4
Solution: Statement 3 is incorrect: When a stimulus is applied, the membrane at that site becomes freely permeable to Na+ not K+ leading to a rapid influx of Na+ not K+ and reversal of polarity

Question:
Read the following statements:
Statement I: Enzymes are divided into 6 classes, each with 4-13 subclasses, named by a 4-digit number
Statement II: Isomerases includes all enzymes catalysing inter-conversion of optical, geometric or positional isomers
Statement III: Lyases catalyze the hydrolysis of ester, ether, peptide, glycosidic, C-C, or C-halide bonds.
Statement IV: Ligases are enzymes catalysing the linking together of two compounds, such as joining C-O, C-S, C-N, or P-O bonds.
Select the correct option:
Options: Statements I, II, and IV are correct
Statements II and III are correct
Statements I and III are correct
All statements are correct
Solution: Statement III is Incorrect: This definition belongs to Hydrolases. Lyases catalyze the removal of groups leaving double bonds.

Question:
Statement I: Light saturation for photosynthesis occurs at 50% of the full sunlight.
Statement II: C3 plants show CO2 saturation at about 360 µlL-1, while C4 plants are saturated only beyond 450 µlL-1
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution:
Statement I is Incorrect: Light saturation occurs at roughly 10% of full sunlight
Statement II is Incorrect: The values are swapped. C4 plants show saturation at about 360µ lL-1, while C3 plants respond to higher concentrations and saturate only beyond 450µ lL-1

Question:
Assertion (A): Archaebacteria differ from other bacteria in having a different cell wall structure
Reason (R): This feature is responsible for their survival in extreme conditions
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: The Assertion is correct. The Reason correctly links this structural difference to their functional ability to withstand extreme heat, salinity, etc.

Question:
Select the correct set of answer among the following:
Statement I: The number of chloroplasts varies from 1 per cell of the Chlamydomonas, a blue green alga to 20-40 per cell in the mesophyll
Statement II: The inner membrane of the mitochondria forms a number of infoldings called the cisternae
Statement III: Lysosomes, a part of the GERL complex, are membrane bound vesicular structures formed by the process of packaging in the golgi apparatus
Statement IV (ER): The ER often shows ribosomes attached to its inner luminal surface
Options: I-True, II-True, III-False, IV-False
I-False, II-False, III-True, IV-False
I-False, II-True, III-True, IV-True
I-True, II-False, III-True, IV-False
Solution:
Statement I, II and IV are False and cam be corrected as:
(I) The number of chloroplasts varies from 1 per cell of the Chlamydomonas, a green alga to 20-40 per cell in the mesophyll
(II) The inner membrane of the mitochondria forms a number of infoldings called the cristae
(IV) Ribosomes are attached to the outer surface of the ER, not the inner luminal surface.

Question:
Read the following four statements (I-IV) and identify the correct sequence of True (T) or False (F):
Statement I: The primary acceptor of CO2 in C3 plants is a 2-carbon ketose sugar called Ribulose bisphosphate (RuBP).
Statement II: The ATP and NADPH produced in the light reaction are used in the biosynthetic phase (dark reaction) occurring in the stroma
Statement III: C4 plants are special because they tolerate higher temperatures and show a response to high light intensities
Statement IV: The current availability of CO2 levels is limiting to the C4 plants.
Options: I-T, II-F, III-F, IV-F
I-T, II-T, III-F, IV-F
I-F, II-T, III-T, IV-F
I-F, II-T, III-F, IV-T
Solution: Statement I is False: The primary acceptor in C3 plants (RuBP) is a 5-carbon ketose sugar, not 2-carbon.

Question:
Statement I: In a telocentric chromosome, the centromere is situated in the middle forming two equal arms
Statement II: Sometimes a few chromosomes have non-staining secondary constrictions at a constant location, which gives the appearance of a small fragment called the satellite.
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement I is Incorrect: A chromosome with the centromere in the middle is metacentric. A telocentric chromosome has a terminal centromere.

Question:
Read the following statements and choose the correct option:
Statement I: In hypogynous flowers, the gynoecium occupies the highest position while other parts are situated below it; the ovary is said to be superior
Statement II: Mustard, China rose, and Plum are examples of hypogynous flowers
Statement III: In epigynous flowers, the margin of the thalamus grows upward enclosing the ovary completely, and other parts arise above the ovary; the ovary is said to be inferior
Statement IV: In perigynous flowers, the gynoecium is situated in the centre and other parts are on the rim of the thalamus almost at the same level; the ovary is said to be half-inferior
Options: Statements I and II are correct
Statements III and IV are correct
Statements I, III and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: Mustard, China rose, and Brinjal are examples of hypogynous flowers

Question:
State whether the following statements are True (T) or False (F):
1. The metabolic fate of pyruvate depends on the cellular needs and oxygen availability
2. In plants, glucose is derived from sucrose, which is the end product of photosynthesis, or from storage carbohydrates
3. The enzyme hexokinase facilitates the phosphorylation of glucose alone and not fructose
4. One molecule of glucose yields a net gain of 38 ATP molecules during glycolysis alone
Options: I-T, II-F, III-F, IV-F
I-T, II-T, III-F, IV-T
I-T, II-T, III-F, IV-F
I-F, II-T, III-F, IV-T
Solution: Glucose and fructose are phosphorylated to give rise to glucose-6 phosphate by the activity of the enzyme hexokinase. Glycolysis alone yields a net gain of 2 ATP (and 2 NADH). The figure 38 (or 36) refers to the complete aerobic respiration of glucose.

Question:
State whether the following statements are True (T) or False (F):
Statement I: About 97% of O2 is transported by RBCs in the blood
Statement II: The remaining 3% of O2 is carried in a dissolved state through the plasma
Statement III: Nearly 70% of CO2 is transported as carbamino-haemoglobin
Statement IV: Carbonic anhydrase is present in very high concentration in the plasma and minute quantities in RBCs
Options: I-T, II-F, III-F, IV-F
I-T, II-T, III-F, IV-F
I-F, II-F, III-T, IV-T
I-F, II-T, III-F, IV-T
Solution:
Statement III is False: Nearly 70% of CO2 is transported as bicarbonate HCO3. Carbamino-haemoglobin accounts for only 20-25%.
Statement IV is False: The enzyme carbonic anhydrase is found in very high concentration in RBCs and minute quantities in the plasma (the reverse of the statement).

Question:
Statement I: The glenoid cavity is a depression in the pelvic girdle which articulates with the head of the femur
Statement II: The acetabulum is a depression in the pectoral girdle which articulates with the head of the humerus.
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution:
Statement I is Incorrect: The glenoid cavity is in the pectoral girdle (scapula) and articulates with the humerus.
Statement II is Incorrect: The acetabulum is in the pelvic girdle (coxal bone) and articulates with the femur. The statements have swapped the locations.

Question:
Study the statements (1-3) given below and select the correct one w.r.t. restriction enzymes:
Statement 1: Today we know more than 900 restriction enzymes that have been isolated from over 230 strains of bacteria
Statement 2: All restriction enzymes recognize same recognition sequences
Statement 3: They add methyl groups to DNA for restricting the growth of bacteriophage
Options: Only 1 and 3
Only 1
Only 1 and 2
Only 3
Solution: Statement 2 is incorrect: All restriction enzymes recognize different recognition sequences.

Question:
Statement I: Mendel published his work on inheritance of characters in 1866 but for several reasons, it remained unrecognised till 1900
Statement II: In 1900, three Scientists (de Vries, Correns and von Tschermak) independently rediscovered Mendel’s results on the inheritance of characters.
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement I is incorrect: Mendel published his work on inheritance of characters in 1865 but for several reasons, it remained unrecognised till 1900

Question:
Read the following four statements (I-IV) regarding the mechanics of Meiosis and select the correct sequence of True (T) or False (F).
Statement I: Meiosis II is initiated immediately after cytokinesis, usually before the chromosomes have fully elongated
Statement II: In Metaphase I, the bivalent chromosomes align on the equatorial plate
Statement III: The splitting of the centromere takes place during Anaphase I allowing homologous chromosomes to separate
Statement IV: Meiosis II resembles a normal mitosis
Options: I-T, II-F, III-F, IV-F
I-T, II-T, III-F, IV-T
I-F, II-F, III-T, IV-T
I-F, II-T, III-F, IV-T
Solution: Statement III is False: Splitting of the centromere occurs in Anaphase II. In Anaphase I, centromeres do not split; homologous chromosomes separate while sister chromatids remain attached.

Question:
Evaluate the following statements regarding the theories of Hugo de Vries and Darwin:
Statement I: Hugo de Vries based his work on the evening primrose (Oenothera lamarckiana)
Statement II: According to de Vries, mutations are small and directional
Statement III: Darwinian variations are small and directional
Statement IV: De Vries believed that mutation caused speciation and hence called it saltation
Options: I-T, II-F, III-F, IV-F
I-T, II-T, III-F, IV-F
I-T, II-F, III-T, IV-T
I-T, II-T, III-F, IV-T
Solution: Statement II is False: De Vries believed mutations were random and directionless.

Question:
Select the correct answer after studying the following statements for Cyclostomata:
Statement I: Some living members of the class Cyclostomata are ectoparasites on all fishes
Statement II: They have an elongated body bearing 6-15 pairs of gill slits for respiration
Statement III: Cyclostomes have a sucking and circular mouth with powerful jaws
Statement IV: The body is devoid of scales and paired fins
Options: Statements I, II and III are correct
Statements II and IV are correct
Statements II and III are correct
All statements are correct
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: All living members of the class Cyclostomata are ectoparasites on some fishes
Statement III is incorrect: Cyclostomes have a sucking and circular mouth without jaws (Agnatha)

Question:
Consider the following four statements regarding specific examples of Genera and Families:
Statement 1: Potato and brinjal are two different species but both belong to the genus Solanum

Statement 2: The genus Panthera includes lion (leo), leopard (pardus), and tiger (tigris)

Statement 3: The family Felidae includes the genus Panthera but excludes the genus Felis

Statement 4: Three different genera Solanum, Petunia, and Datura are placed in the family Solanaceae

How many of the above statements are correct?
Options: One
Two
Three
Four
Solution: Statement 3 is Incorrect. Family Felidae includes both Panthera (big cats) and Felis (cats)

Question:
Assertion (A): Diatoms are used in polishing and filtration of oils and syrups
Reason (R): The soil containing their cell wall deposits is gritty in nature
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: The Assertion is correct. The Reason explains why they are used for polishing: the silica-embedded walls pile up to form diatomaceous earth, which is gritty (abrasive), making it suitable for polishing and filtration.

Question:

Question:
Assertion (A): The structure of amino acids changes in solutions of different pH.
Reason (R): The variable R-group in amino acids is always ionizable and responsible for the zwitterionic nature.

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer:
Statement I: IUDs are presently available as copper releasing IUDs (CuT), non-medicated IUDs (LNG-20) and hormone releasing IUDs (Lippes loop)
Statement II: IUDs increase phagocytosis of sperms within the uterus
Statement III: Hormone-releasing IUDs make the uterus suitable for implantation and the cervix hostile to the sperms
Statement IV: Copper ions (Cu2+) released by some IUDs suppress sperm motility and the fertilising capacity of sperms
Options: Statements I and III are correct
Statements II and IV are correct
Statements I, II and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: IUDs are presently available as non-medicated IUDs (Lippes loop), copper releasing IUDs (CuT) and hormone releasing IUDs (LNG-20)
Statement IV is incorrect: Hormone-releasing IUDs make the uterus unsuitable for implantation.

Question:
Statement I: In Citrus, stamens are united into two bundles, a condition known as diadelphous
Statement II: In Pea, stamens are united into a single bundle, a condition known as monadelphous

Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct

Statement I is incorrect: Citrus shows polyadelphous condition (many bundles)

Statement II is incorrect: Pea shows diadelphous condition (two bundles, 9+1 arrangement); Monadelphous is seen in China rose.

Question:
Statement I: The primary acceptor of CO2 in the Calvin cycle (C3 pathway) is a 5-carbon ketose sugar called Ribulose bisphosphate (RuBP)
Statement II: The first stable product of the Calvin cycle is a 2-carbon compound called Phosphoglycolate
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is Incorrect: The first stable product in the Calvin cycle is a 3-carbon organic acid, 3-phosphoglyceric acid (3-PGA). Phosphoglycolate is associated with photorespiration.

Question:
Assertion (A): Salvia and Mustard show variation in the length of filaments within a flower
Reason (R): A sterile stamen is called a staminode
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: The definition of a staminode does not explain why filament lengths vary in Salvia. These are two distinct facts about the Androecium

Question: Identify what the given diagram indicates?
Options: Convergent evolution
Divergent evolution
Recapitulation
Parallel evolution
Solution: The correct matching is: Divergent evolution

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer:
Statement I: According to Hugo de Vries, mutations are random and directionless, while Darwinian variations are small and directional
Statement II: For de Vries, evolution was a gradual process, whereas Darwin believed mutation caused speciation and called it saltation
Statement III: The total genes and their alleles in a population is called the gene library
Statement IV: The Hardy-Weinberg Principle states that allele frequencies in a population are stable and constant from generation to generation in the absence of evolutionary influences
Options: Statements I, III, and IV are correct
Statements I and II are correct
Statements II, III, and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution:
Statement II is incorrect: It was Hugo de Vries who believed mutation caused speciation and called it saltation (single-step large mutation), and he believed evolution was jerky, not gradual. Darwin believed in gradualism.
Statement III is incorrect: The total genes and their alleles in a population is called the gene pool

Question:
Consider the following statements:
Statement I: The pulmonary circulation starts by the pumping of deoxygenated blood by the right ventricle which is carried to the lungs where it is oxygenated
Statement II: The systemic circulation provides nutrients, O2, and other essential substances to the tissues and takes CO2 and other harmful substances away for elimination
Statement III: A unique vascular connection exists between the digestive tract and the liver called the renal portal system
Statement IV: The hepatic portal vein carries blood from the liver to the intestine
Which of the above statements are CORRECT?
Options: I, II and III
II, III and IV
I and II only
I and IV only
Solution:
Statement III is incorrect: A unique vascular connection exists between the digestive tract and the liver called the hepatic portal system
Statement IV is Incorrect: The hepatic portal vein carries blood from the intestine to the liver (not liver to intestine) before it is delivered to the systemic circulation

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer:
Statement I: Walter Sutton and Theodore Boveri noted that the behaviour of chromosomes was parallel to the behaviour of genes
Statement II: Chromosomes, as well as genes, occur in pairs; the two alleles of a gene pair are located on homologous sites on homologous chromosomes
Statement III: Sutton and Boveri argued that the pairing and separation of a pair of chromosomes would lead to the segregation of a pair of factors they carried
Statement IV: The knowledge of chromosomal segregation was united with Mendelian principles by Thomas Hunt Morgan to call it the Chromosomal Theory of Inheritance
Options: Statements I and III are correct
Statements II and IV are correct
Statements I, II, and III are correct
All statements are correct
Solution: Statement IV is incorrect: Morgan provided experimental verification, the theory was originally united and named by Sutton and Boveri. Morgan’s work came later to prove the theory using Drosophila.

Question:
Statement I: Human insulin is synthesized as a pro-hormone which contains an extra stretch called the C-peptide
Statement II: The C-peptide is removed during maturation into insulin and is present in the mature insulin available in the blood
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is Incorrect: The C-peptide is removed during maturation; therefore, it is not present in the mature insulin molecule. This was the main challenge in producing functional insulin via rDNA technology.

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer:
Statement I: Non-albuminous or ex-albuminous seeds have no residual endosperm as it is completely consumed during embryo development (e.g., Pea, Groundnut).
Statement II: Albuminous seeds retain a part of endosperm as it is not completely used up during embryo development (e.g., Wheat, Maize, Castor).
Statement III: In some seeds such as black pepper and beet, remnants of nucellus are also persistent; this residual, persistent nucellus is the perisperm.
Statement IV: The micropyle remains as a small pore in the seed coat to allow the entry of water and oxygen during germination
Options: Statements I and II are correct
Statements II, III and IV are correct
Statements I, II and III are correct
All statements are correct
Solution: All statements are correct.

A-ii, B-i, C-iv, D-iii
A-ii, B-iv, C-i, D-iii
A-i, B-iii, C-ii, D-iv
Solution: The correct matching is: A-ii, B-iv, C-i, D-iii

Question:
Assertion (A): Materials to be packaged in the form of vesicles from the ER fuse with the trans face of the Golgi apparatus
Reason (R): Materials move from the cis face to the trans face within the Golgi cisternae for modification and packaging
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution:
Assertion is False: Materials from the ER fuse with the cis (forming) face, not the trans face.
Reason is True: The direction of flow for processing is indeed from the cis (convex) face to the trans (concave) face, where they are eventually released.

Question:
Which of the following statements are correct regarding glomerular filtration?
(I) The process of glomerular filtration is also known as ultrafiltration
(II) The glomerular capillary blood pressure is responsible for the filtration of blood
(III) Filtration slits in the glomerulus are formed by the endothelial cells
(IV) Almost all plasma constituents including proteins pass into the Bowman’s capsule
Which of the following options is the most appropriate?
Options: (I) and (II) are correct
(III) and (IV) are correct
(I) and (III) are correct
(II) and (IV) are correct
Solution:
Statement III is incorrect: Filtration slits are formed by podocytes, not endothelial cells
Statement IV is incorrect: Proteins do not pass into the Bowman’s capsule

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer
Statement I: Amphibians possess a three-chambered heart (two auricles and one ventricle), while all reptiles possess a four-chambered heart
Statement II: In Amphibians, the skin is moist (with scales) and the eyes have eyelids
Statement III: Reptiles are mostly terrestrial animals and their body is covered by dry and cornified skin, epidermal scales or scutes
Statement IV: A tympanum represents the ear in both amphibians and reptiles
Options: Statements I, II and III are correct
Statements II, III and IV are correct
Statements I and IV are correct
Statements III and IV are correct
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: Most reptiles have a three-chambered heart; however, crocodiles are the exception with a four-chambered heart.
Statement II is incorrect: In Amphibians, the skin is moist (without scales) and the eyes have eyelids

Question:

Reason (R): Stability is inversely proportional to the energy status of the molecule; since the transition state has higher energy than both substrate and product, it is unstable.
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.
Solution: Both A and R are true and R correctly explains A.

Question:
Assertion (A): The selection of recombinants using the pBR322 requires simultaneous plating on two different antibiotic-containing media
Reason (R): Recombinants lose resistance to one antibiotic due to the insertion of foreign DNA but retain resistance to the other antibiotic
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both are true. For example, if you insert DNA at the BamH I site, you must plate on ampicillin to find transformants and then replica-plate onto tetracycline to identify which of those are recombinants (the ones that die on tetracycline)

Question:
Consider the following four statements regarding the different types of simple epithelium:
Statement 1: Squamous epithelium is made of a single thin layer of flattened cells with irregular boundaries
Statement 2: Cuboidal epithelium is composed of a single layer of cube-like cells and is commonly found in ducts of glands
Statement 3: Columnar epithelium consists of a single layer of tall and slender cells with their nuclei located at the very top of the cell
Statement 4: If the columnar or cuboidal cells bear cilia on their free surface, they are called ciliated epithelium
Options: Statement 1 and 2
Statement 3 only
Statement 4 only
Statement 2 and 3
Solution: Statement 3 is Incorrect: In columnar epithelium, the nuclei are located at the base of the cell, not at the top.

Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct

Question:
Assertion (A): The content of the nucleolus is continuous with the rest of the nucleoplasm.
Reason (R): The nucleolus is not a membrane-bound structure.
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.
Solution:
Assertion is True: There is no barrier between the nucleolus and the nucleoplasm.
Reason is True: It lacks a limiting membrane.
Explanation: The absence of a boundary membrane (Reason) is the direct cause of the physical continuity (Assertion).

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer:

Statement II: Appearance of dry, scaly lesions on various parts of the body such as skin, nails, and scalp is the main symptom of ringworm

Statement IV: For diseases like malaria and filariasis, the most important preventive measure is the elimination of vectors and their breeding places
Options: Statements I and II are correct
Statements III and IV are correct

Question:
Assertion (A): In 1997, an American company was granted a patent on Basmati rice by the US Patent and Trademark Office
Reason (R): The "new" variety of Basmati was actually derived from Indian farmer’s varieties by crossing them with semi-dwarf varieties
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both statements are true. The company claimed the variety was a "novelty," but since it used existing Indian germplasm, it was considered an act of biopiracy. The reason correctly identifies the source of the variety but is a statement of fact rather than an explanation of the legal granting of the patent itself.

Question:
Assertion (A): Glucocorticoids, such as cortisol, stimulate gluconeogenesis, lipolysis, and proteolysis
Reason (R): Cortisol is also involved in maintaining the cardiovascular system and kidney functions
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both statements are factually correct functions of cortisol. However, the fact that it maintains the cardiovascular system (Reason) does not explain why or how it stimulates gluconeogenesis (Assertion). They are separate functions.

Question:

Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct

Question:

A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Assertion is False: Osteichthyes are mostly oviparous (egg-laying) and fertilisation is usually external.

Question:

Reason (R): High blood pressure can lead to heart diseases and also affects vital organs like the brain and kidney.
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Assertion is False: Normal blood pressure is 120/80 mmHg. A reading of 140/90 mmHg or higher indicates Hypertension.

Question:
Analyze the statements below regarding the Counter Current Mechanism:
Statement I: Mammals have the ability to produce a concentrated urine using the Henle's loop and vasa recta
Statement II: The flow of filtrate in the two limbs of Henle's loop is in opposite directions and thus forms a counter current
Statement III: The osmolarity of the medullary interstitium decreases from the cortex to the inner medulla
Statement IV: NaCl is transported by the ascending limb of Henle's loop which is exchanged with the descending limb of vasa recta
How many of the above statements are INCORRECT?
Options: One
Two
Three
Four
Solution: Statement III is Incorrect: The osmolarity increases (not decreases) from the cortex to the inner medulla (from 300 to 1200 mOsmol/L)

Question:
Assertion (A): In Gymnosperms, the seeds are naked and remain exposed both before and after fertilization
Reason (R): The ovules in Gymnosperms are not enclosed by any ovary wall
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: The Assertion is correct. The term "Gymnosperm" literally means "naked seed". The Reason correctly explains why the seeds are naked: the absence of an enclosing ovary wall means the ovules (and subsequent seeds) are exposed.

Question: Which of the following describes the given graph correctly?
Options: Endothermic reaction with energy – A in the presence of enzyme and B in the absence of enzyme
Exothermic reaction with energy – A in the presence of enzyme and B in the absence of enzyme
Endothermic reaction with energy – A in the absence of enzyme and B in the presence of enzyme
Exothermic reaction with energy – A in the absence of enzyme and B in the presence of enzyme
Solution: The graph shows exothermic reaction. The reaction graph depicted by A states its occurrence in the presence of enzyme as it lowers down the activation energy substantially. The B graph shows this reaction occurring in the absence of enzyme when activation energy is quite high.

Question:
Match the component in Column I with its structural arrangement/description in Column II:
Column IColumn II
A.Axoneme(1)9 + 0 arrangement of microtubules
B.Centriole(2)9 + 2 arrangement of microtubules
C.Nuclear Envelope(3)Double membrane with perinuclear space
D.Radial Spokes(4)Connect peripheral doublets to central sheath
Solution:
Axoneme: The core of cilia/flagella possessing a 9 + 2 array (9 peripheral doublets + 1 central pair).
Centriole: Possesses a 9 + 0 array (9 peripheral triplets + no central microtubules).
Nuclear Envelope: Consists of two parallel membranes with a space called the perinuclear space (10-50 nm).

Radial Spokes: In cilia/flagella, these connect the peripheral doublets to the central sheath.

Question: In the given diagram of mechanism of breathing, what does A, B and C depict?
Options: A–Air goes inside to lungs, B–Ribs and sternum returned to original position, C– Diaphragm contracted
A–Air expelled from lungs, B–Ribs and sternum returned to original position, C–Diaphragm relaxed and arched upward
A–Air expelled from lungs, B–Ribs and sternum go upward, C–Diaphragm relaxed and arched upward
A–Air goes inside to lungs, B–Ribs and sternum go upward, C–Diaphragm relaxed and arched upward
Solution: Relaxation of the diaphragm and the inter-costal muscles returns the diaphragm and sternum to their normal positions and reduce the thoracic volume and thereby the pulmonary volume. This leads to an increase in intra-pulmonary pressure to slightly above the atmospheric pressure causing the expulsion of air from the lungs, i.e., expiration

Assertion A: Fibroblasts, macrophages, and mast cells are present in the areolar tissue.
Reason R: Areolar tissue serves as a support framework for epithelium
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both statements are textually correct according to NCERT. However, the presence of specific cells (fibroblasts, etc.) is not the explanation for why the tissue serves as a support framework for epithelium. The support function is due to its location and structural composition (loose arrangement), not just the list of cells it contains.

Question:
Consider the following statements regarding the initial step of urine formation:
Statement I: The glomerular capillary blood pressure causes filtration of blood through three layers: the endothelium, the basement membrane, and the epithelium of Bowman's capsule.
Statement II: The epithelial cells of Bowman's capsule called podocytes are arranged in an intricate manner so as to leave some minute spaces called filtration slits or slit pores.
Statement III: Blood is filtered so finely through these membranes that almost all the constituents of the plasma including the proteins pass onto the lumen of the Bowman's capsule.
Statement IV: GFR in a healthy individual is approximately 125 ml/minute, i.e., 180 litres per day.
Which of the above statements are CORRECT?
Options: I, II and III
I, II and IV
II, III and IV
All of the above
Solution: Statement III is Incorrect: The process is called ultrafiltration because almost all constituents of plasma pass through except the proteins. Proteins are too large to pass through the filtration slits.

Question:
Analyze the statements below regarding ATP synthesis:
Statement I: The energy of oxidation-reduction utilised for the production of the proton gradient is required for oxidative phosphorylation
Statement II: Complex V consists of two major components, F1 and F0
Statement III: The F1 headpiece is an integral membrane protein complex that forms the channel through which protons cross the membrane
Statement IV: The passage of protons through the channel is coupled to the catalytic site of the F1 component for the production of ATP
Which combination represents the CORRECT statements?
Options: I, II and III
I, II and IV
II and IV only
I and III only
Solution: Statement III is Incorrect: The F0 component is the integral membrane protein that forms the channel. The F1 headpiece is a peripheral membrane protein complex where ATP synthesis occurs

Question:
Assertion (A): The diffusion membrane is less than a millimetre in thickness
Reason (R): The diffusion membrane consists of only two layers: the alveolar epithelium and the capillary endothelium
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Reason is False: The diffusion membrane consists of three layers: the thin squamous epithelium of alveoli, the endothelium of alveolar capillaries, and the basement substance in between them.

Question:
Read the following statements carefully and select the correct answer among the following
Statement I: Many marine fish are infested with endoparasitic copepods
Statement II: Abingdon tortoise in Galapagos Islands became extinct within a decade after goats were introduced on the island, apparently due to the greater browsing efficiency of the goats
Statement III: Monarch butterfly is highly distasteful to its predator (bird) because of a special chemical present in its body, which it acquires during its caterpillar stage by feeding on a poisonous weed
Statement IV: In accordance with their life styles, parasites evolved special adaptations such as the loss of unnecessary sense organs, presence of adhesive organs or suckers to cling on to the host, loss of high reproductive capacity and efficient, well-developed digestive system
Options: Statements I and III are correct
Statements II and III are correct
Statements II, III and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: Many marine fish are infested with ectoparasitic copepods
Statement IV is incorrect: In accordance with their life styles, parasites evolved special adaptations such as the loss of unnecessary sense organs, presence of adhesive organs or suckers to cling on to the host, loss of digestive system and high reproductive capacity

Question:
State whether the following statements are True (T) or False (F):
I. Adrenal medullary hormones can increase the cardiac output
II. Congestive heart failure is so called because congestion of the lungs is one of the main symptoms of this disease
III. The P-wave in an ECG represents the depolarization of the ventricles
IV. Coronary Artery Disease (CAD) is caused by deposits of calcium, fat, and fibrous tissue which makes the arterial lumen wider
Options: I-T, II-F, III-F, IV-F
I-T, II-T, III-F, IV-F
I-F, II-F, III-T, IV-T
I-F, II-T, III-F, IV-T
Solution:
Statement III - False: P-wave represents depolarization of the atria (not ventricles).
Statement IV - False: The deposits make the arterial lumen narrower, not wider.

Question:
Analyze the statements below regarding Haemodialysis:
Statement I: Blood drained from a convenient vein is pumped into a dialysing unit after adding an anticoagulant like heparin
Statement II: The unit contains a coiled cellophane tube surrounded by a fluid (dialysing fluid) having absolutely the same composition as that of plasma
Statement III: The porous cellophane membrane allows the passage of molecules based on concentration gradient
Statement IV: The cleared blood is pumped back to the body through an artery after adding anti-heparin to it
How many of the above statements are INCORRECT?
Options: One
Two
Three
None
Solution:
Statement I: Blood drained from a convenient artery not vein is pumped into a dialysing unit after adding an anticoagulant like heparin
Statement II is incorrect: The unit contains a coiled cellophane tube surrounded by a fluid (dialysing fluid) having the same composition as that of plasma except the nitrogenous wastes
Statement IV: The cleared blood is pumped back to the body through a vein not an artery after adding anti-heparin to it

Question:
Assertion (A): Cornelius van Niel inferred that the O2 evolved by the green plant comes from H2O, not from carbon dioxide
Reason (R): In purple and green sulphur bacteria, the hydrogen donor is H2S, and the oxidation product is sulphur or sulphate depending on the organism and not O2
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both statements are correct but the reason does not correctly explains the assertion. Assertion is about green plants and reason is about purple and green sulphur bacteria

Question:
Statement I: The cells of the secondary cortex (phelloderm) which are formed by the cork cambium are examples of dedifferentiated cells
Statement II: The formation of meristems like the interfascicular cambium and cork cambium from fully differentiated parenchyma cells is an example of dedifferentiation
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement I is Incorrect: The cells of the secondary cortex are redifferentiated cells. They are produced by the dedifferentiated tissue (cork cambium) but have lost the capacity to divide again to perform specific functions.

Question:
Consider the following four statements regarding the Hypothalamus:
Statement 1: It contains several groups of neurosecretory cells called nuclei which produce hormones
Statement 2: The hormones produced by the hypothalamus regulate the synthesis and secretion of pituitary hormones
Statement 3: GnRH stimulates the pituitary synthesis and release of gonadotrophins.
Statement 4: Somatostatin from the hypothalamus stimulates the release of growth hormone from the pituitary
How many of the above statements are correct?
Options: One
Two
Three
Four
Solution: Statement 4 is Incorrect: Somatostatin inhibits the release of growth hormone from the pituitary (it is an inhibitory hormone).

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer
Statement I: Rice is an important food grain, the presence of which goes back thousands of years in Asia’s agricultural history
Statement II: There are an estimated 200,000 varieties of rice in India alone, making it one of the richest in the world
Statement III: In 1979, an American company got patent rights on Basmati rice through the US Patent and Trademark Office
Statement IV: This patent allowed the company to sell a ‘new’ variety of Basmati in the US and abroad, which was actually derived from Indian farmer’s varieties
Options: Statements I and II are correct
Statements III and IV are correct
Statements I, II, and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution: Statement III is incorrect: In 1997, an American company got patent rights on Basmati rice through the US Patent and Trademark Office

Question:
Statement I: The flower of Solanaceae is zygomorphic and the calyx is usually deciduous (falls off)
Statement II: The gynoecium is monocarpellary and syncarpous with a superior ovary
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: Solanaceae flowers are actinomorphic (radial symmetry), and the calyx is usually persistent (remains attached, e.g., in Brinjal/Tomato).
Statement II is incorrect: The gynoecium is bicarpellary and syncarpous with a superior ovary

Question:
Assertion (A): Larger and more numerous nucleoli are present in cells actively carrying out protein synthesis.
Reason (R): The nucleolus is the site for active ribosomal RNA (rRNA) synthesis, which is essential for ribosome formation.
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.
Solution: Since ribosomes (made of rRNA and proteins) are the protein-synthesizing machinery, the need for protein synthesis (Assertion) is directly supported by the function of the nucleolus (Reason). Thus, R explains A.

Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: The acid-soluble pool contains small biomolecules that dissolve in the trichloroacetic acid solution used for grinding

Question:
Match the type of Co-factor in Column I with its specific characteristic/example in Column II.
Column IColumn II
A.Prosthetic Group(1)Protein portion of the enzyme
B.Co-enzyme(2)Organic compounds loosely bound to the enzyme
C.Metal Ion(3)Organic compounds tightly bound to the apoenzyme
D.Apoenzyme(4)Forms coordination bonds with side chains at the active site (e.g., Zinc)
Solution:
The correct matching is: A-3, B-2, C-4, D-1
Prosthetic Groups: Are tightly bound (e.g., Haem).
Co-enzymes: Association is transient, usually occurring during catalysis (e.g., Vitamins like Niacin).
Metal Ions: Form coordination bonds with the enzyme and substrate (e.g., Zinc in carboxypeptidase).
Apoenzyme: The protein part of the enzyme

Question:
Assertion (A): In Griffith's experiments, mice injected with a mixture of heat-killed S-strain and live R-strain bacteria died of pneumonia
Reason (R): The R-strain bacteria had transitioned into S-strain by absorbing some "transforming principle" from the heat-killed S-strain
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Griffith observed that heat-killed S-strain alone didn't kill the mice, and neither did live R-strain. However, when combined, the live R-strain "transformed" into virulent S-strain by picking up genetic material (the transforming principle) from the dead S-strain, causing the mice to die.

Question:

Reason (R): In these cases, karyokinesis is not followed by cytokinesis
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both statements are true and R provides the direct cause for the condition described in A..

Question:
Assertion (A): The alimentary canal of a frog is short
Reason (R): Frogs are carnivores and hence the length of the intestine is reduced
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both Assertion and Reason are correct. Herbivores typically need longer intestines to digest cellulose, whereas carnivores (like frogs) have shorter intestines because meat is easier to digest. Thus, R is the correct explanation for A.

Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II: Recombinant DNA is directly injected into the nucleus of an animal cell by the process of microinjection

Question:
Assertion (A): Angiotensin II, being a powerful vasodilator, decreases the glomerular blood pressure
Reason (R): Angiotensin I activates the adrenal medulla to release Aldosterone
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
Both A and R are false
Solution:
Assertion is False: Angiotensin II is a powerful vasoconstrictor (not vasodilator), and it increases the glomerular blood pressure.
Reason (R): Angiotensin II activates the adrenal cortex not medulla to release Aldosterone

Question:
Select the correct answer after studying the following statements:
Statement I: Geitonogamy is the transfer of pollen grains from the anther to the stigma of another flower of the same plant
Statement II: Functional geitonogamy is a type of autogamy since the pollen grains come from the same plant
Statement III: Genetically, geitonogamy is similar to cross-pollination involving a pollinating agent
Statement IV: Xenogamy is the only type of pollination which during pollination brings genetically different types of pollen grains to the stigma
Options: Statements I and IV are correct
Statements I, III and IV are correct
Statements II, III and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution:
Statement II: Functional geitonogamy is a type of cross-pollination involving a pollinating agent
Statement III: Genetically, geitonogamy is similar to autogamy since the pollen grains come from the same plant

Question:

Reason (R): In these flowers, the margin of the thalamus grows upward enclosing the ovary completely and getting fused with it, making the ovary inferior
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A. The Assertion is correct (examples of epigynous flowers). The Reason correctly describes the structural arrangement (thalamus enclosing ovary) that defines the epigynous condition and the resulting inferior ovary.

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer
Statements:
I. Areolar tissue is present beneath the skin and serves as a support framework for epithelium
II. Adipose tissue is a loose connective tissue located mainly beneath the skin specialized to store fats
III. The cells of areolar tissue include fibroblasts, macrophages, and mast cells
IV. The excess of nutrients which are not used immediately are converted into fats and stored in the matrix of areolar tissue
Options: Only Statement IV is incorrect
Statements I and IV are incorrect
Statements II and III are incorrect
All statements are correct
Solution: Statement IV is incorrect: The excess nutrients not used immediately are converted into fats and stored in Adipose tissue, not Areolar tissue.

Question:
After studying the following statements select the correct answer among the following:
Statement I: competition is best defined as a process in which the fitness of one species (measured in terms of its ‘r’ the intrinsic rate of increase) is significantly lower in the presence of another species
Statement II: Mycorrhizae are associations between fungi and the leaves of higher plants
Statement III: Carnivores and plants appear to be more adversely affected by competition than herbivores
Statement IV: The Mediterranean orchid Ophrys employs ‘sexual deceit’ to get pollination done by a species of bee
Options: Statements I, II, and III are correct
Statements II and IV are correct
Statements I and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution:
Statement II is incorrect: Mycorrhizae are associations between fungi and the roots of higher plants, not the leaves. The fungi help the plant in the absorption of essential nutrients from the soil.
Statement III is incorrect: Herbivores and plants appear to be more adversely affected by competition than carnivores

Question:
Analyze the statements below:
Statement I: Neutrophils are the most abundant cells and Basophils are the least abundant among WBCs
Statement II: Neutrophils and Monocytes are non-phagocytic cells which destroy foreign organisms entering the body
Statement III: Eosinophils resist infections and are also associated with allergic reactions
Statement IV: Platelets (thrombocytes) are cell fragments produced from megakaryocytes, and their normal count is 15,000 - 35,000 per mm3
Which combination represents the CORRECT statements?
Options: I, II and III only
I and IV only
II, III and IV only
I and III only
Solution:
Statement II is incorrect: Neutrophils and Monocytes are phagocytic cells which destroy foreign organisms entering the body
Statement IV is Incorrect: The normal platelet count is 1,50,000 - 3,50,000 per mm3 (1.5 - 3.5 lakh) not 15,000-35,000.

Question:
Statement I: Lactational amenorrhea is effective as a contraceptive method for as long as the mother continues to breastfeed the child
Statement II: This method is based on the fact that ovulation and the menstrual cycle do not occur during the period of intense lactation following parturition
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement I is Incorrect: According to NCERT, this method has been reported to be effective only up to a maximum period of six months following parturition, not indefinitely as long as breastfeeding continues.

Question:

Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Assertion is Incorrect: Gymnosperms lack vessels in their xylem. Their main water-conducting elements are tracheids. (Presence of vessels is a characteristic feature of Angiosperms).

Question:

Question:
Analyze the statements below regarding Actin and Myosin:

Statement II: A complex protein Tropomyosin is distributed at regular intervals on the troponin.
Statement III: The globular head of meromyosin is an active ATPase enzyme and has binding sites for ATP and active sites for actin
Statement IV: The heavy meromyosin (HMM) component includes the head and a short arm, which projects outwards at a regular distance and angle
How many of the above statements are INCORRECT?
Options: None
One
Two
Three
Solution: Statement II is Incorrect: A complex protein Troponin is distributed at regular intervals on the Tropomyosin (not the other way around).

Question:

Question:
Read the following statements and choose the correct option:
Statement I: Aestivation is the mode of arrangement of sepals or petals in the floral bud with respect to the other members of the same whorl
Statement II: In valvate aestivation, sepals or petals just touch one another at the margin without overlapping, as seen in Calotropis
Statement III: In twisted aestivation, one margin of the appendage overlaps that of the next one and so on, as seen in China rose and Cotton
Statement IV: In imbricate aestivation, the margins overlap one another but not in any particular direction, as seen in Pea and Bean flowers
Options: Statements II and IV are correct
Statements I and IV are correct
Statements I, II and III are correct
All statements are correct
Solution: Statement IV is incorrect: The description of imbricate aestivation (irregular overlap) is correct for Cassia and Gulmohur. Pea and Bean flowers exhibit Vexillary (or papilionaceous) aestivation, not imbricate

Question:
Consider the following four statements:
1. All the carbon compounds obtained from living tissues are called biomolecules.
2. The acid-soluble pool has an average elemental composition for hydrogen of 0.5%.
3. Secondary metabolites, such as rubber and gums, are found in all plant, fungal, and microbial cells.
4. Ash contains both organic and inorganic compounds
Which of the above statements are incorrect?
Options: Statements 1 and 2
Statements 1, 2, and 4
Statements 2, 3 and 4
Statements 1 and 4
Solution:
Statements 2, 3 and 4 are incorrect and can be corrected as:
Statement 2: The NCERT table on elemental analysis shows the average percentage of hydrogen in the human body's acid-soluble pool as 9.5%
Statement 3: Secondary metabolites (like rubber, gums, alkaloids, terpenes) have no apparent function for the organism, but their presence is not uniform; they are characteristic of only certain cells or species (e.g., fungi, microbes, and certain plants) but not all.
Statement 4: Ash contains inorganic elements (like calcium, magnesium etc)

Question:
Statement I: Birds are homeothermic (warm-blooded) animals and are able to maintain a constant body temperature
Statement II: In birds, the oil gland is present all over the skin to keep the feathers waterproof
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: In Aves, the skin is dry and devoid of glands, except for the oil gland at the base of the tail

Question:
Assertion (A): Testis performs dual functions as a primary sex organ as well as an endocrine gland
Reason (R): Leydig cells are located inside the seminiferous tubules along with the male germ cells
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: Reason is False: Leydig cells are located in the intertubular spaces (outside the seminiferous tubules), not inside them. The cells inside the tubules are male germ cells and Sertoli cells.

Question:

Question:
Statement I: Pleiotropy occurs when a single gene can exhibit multiple phenotypic expressions
Statement II: Phenylketonuria is caused by a mutation in the gene that codes for the enzyme phenylalanine oxidase, leading to mental retardation and skin depigmentation
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is correct
Solution: Statement II is incorrect: Phenylketonuria is caused by a mutation in the gene that codes for the enzyme phenylalanine hydroxylase, leading to mental retardation and skin depigmentation. Phenylketonuria is the classic example of pleiotropy because a single metabolic defect results in multiple, seemingly unrelated symptoms across different organ systems.

Question:
Match the structure in Column I with its specific characteristic or function in Column II:
Column IColumn II
A.Cytoskeleton(1)Active site for ribosomal RNA synthesis
B.Centriole(2)Mechanical support and maintenance of cell shape
C.Nucleolus(3)Emerges from basal bodies
D.Flagella(4)Forms the spindle apparatus during cell division
Solution:
Cytoskeleton: An elaborate network of filamentous proteinaceous structures that provides mechanical support and maintains cell shape.
Centriole: Forms the spindle apparatus (spindle fibres) in animal cells during cell division.

Nucleolus: A non-membrane bound structure that is the site for active ribosomal RNA (rRNA) synthesis.

Flagella: Hair-like outgrowths of the cell membrane that emerge from basal bodies.

Question:
Consider the following statements about the classification of amino acids based on their R-groups:

(B) Lysine is an acidic amino acid because it possesses an ionizable nature.

Question:

Question:

Question:

Statement III: Co-extinction occurs when a host fish species becomes extinct, leading to the extinction of its unique assemblage of parasites
Statement IV: Introduction of the African catfish (Clarias gariepinus) for aquaculture is posing a threat to the indigenous catfishes in our rivers
Options: Statements I, II, and IV are correct
Statements II and III are correct
Statements I and III are correct

Question:

Statement II: Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll and carotenoid pigments which are responsible for trapping light energy essential for photosynthesis
Statement III: The ribosomes of the chloroplasts are larger than the cytoplasmic ribosomes
Statement IV: The primary wall of a young plant cell is capable of growth, which gradually diminishes as the cell matures
How many of the above statements are incorrect?
Options: One
Two
Three
All statements are correct
Solution:

Question:

Reason (R): Humus is a dark-colored amorphous substance that undergoes decomposition at an extremely fast rate through mineralisation
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Solution: The Assertion is true; humus acts as a nutrient reservoir. However, the Reason is false because humus is highly resistant to microbial action and undergoes decomposition at an extremely slow rate, not a fast rate.

Question:

Statement III: Dinoflagellates are mostly marine and photosynthetic, appearing yellow, green, brown, blue, or red depending on the main pigments present

Options: Statements I, II and III are correct
Statements I and IV are correct
Statements I and III are correct
All statements are correct
Solution:
Statement II is incorrect: In diatoms, the cell walls form two thin overlapping shells, which fit together as in a soap box, and are embedded with silica not CaCO3 making them indestructible
Statement IV is incorrect: Diatoms are the chief ‘producers’ in the oceans not Dinoflagellates

Question:

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer:
Statement I: The abdomen in both males and females consists of 10 segments
Statement II: The adults of the common species of cockroach, Periplaneta americana are about 34-53 mm long with wings that extend beyond the tip of the abdomen in females
Statement III: In males, the genital pouch is bounded dorsally by the 9th and 10th terga and ventrally by the 9th sternum
Statement IV: Anal styles are thread-like structures present in females on the 9th segment but are absent in males
Options: Statements I and III are correct
Statements I, II and III are correct
Statements III and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution:
Statement II is incorrect: The adults of the common species of cockroach, Periplaneta americana are about 34-53 mm long with wings that extend beyond the tip of the abdomen in males not females

Question:
Read the following statements and select the correct answer:
Statement I: Alfred Hershey and Martha Chase (1952) worked with bacteriophages and used radioactive phosphorus (32P) to label the protein coat
Statement II: Radioactive sulphur (35S) was used to label the DNA of the bacteriophage
Statement III: After infection and centrifugation, radioactivity was found in the supernatant in the case of cells infected with phages having radioactive protein
Statement IV: Bacteria which were infected with phages that had radioactive DNA were radioactive, indicating that DNA was the material that passed from the virus to the bacteria
Options: Statements I and II are correct
Statements III and IV are correct
Statements I, II, and III are correct
All statements are correct
Solution:
Statement I is incorrect: 32P labels DNA (which contains phosphorus), not proteins.
Statement II is incorrect: 35S labels proteins (which contain sulphur), not DNA.

Question:
Read the following statements regarding Ascomycetes and select the correct set of statements:
Statement I: They are mostly multicellular (e.g., Penicillium) or rarely unicellular (e.g., Yeast) and are saprophytic, decomposers, parasitic or coprophilous
Statement II: The asexual spores are conidia produced exogenously on the special mycelium called conidiophores
Statement III: The sexual spores are called ascospores which are produced exogenously in sac-like asci
Statement IV: The mycelium is branched and septate
Options: Statements II and III are correct
Statements I and III are correct
Statements I, II and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution: Statement III is incorrect: Ascospores are produced endogenously in sac-like asci (unlike conidia which are exogenous)

Question:

Statement I: Males have a pair of testes lying one on each lateral side in the 4th to 6th abdominal segments
Statement II: The characteristic mushroom-shaped gland is present in the 6th -7th abdominal segments in males which functions as an accessory reproductive gland
Statement III: Females consist of two large ovaries, lying laterally in the 2nd to 6th abdominal segments
Statement IV: In females, a pair of spermatheca is present in the 9th segment which opens into the genital chamber
Options: Statements I, II and III are correct
Statements I and IV are correct
Statements II, III and IV are correct
All statements are correct
Solution: Statement IV is incorrect: A pair of spermatheca is present in the 6th segment (not 9th) which opens into the genital chamber.

Question:
